headline: Specialist Doctor Shortage Reaches 5,800 Vacancies Nationwide
context: MOH reports 5,800 unfilled specialist positions across public hospitals as experienced doctors migrate to private sector.

cards.big: 5,800 specialist doctor positions vacant in government hospitals as of January 2026
cards.sub: Oncology cardiology and psychiatry face the most critical shortages

cards.big: 72% of specialists who leave public service cite salary differential as primary reason
cards.sub: Private sector pays specialists 3-5 times more than government scale

cards.big: Patients wait average 6 months for specialist appointments at public hospitals in 2025
cards.sub: Delayed specialist access leads to disease progression and worse outcomes

cards.big: UK NHS introduced specialist retention bonuses of up to GBP20,000 in 2023
cards.sub: Targeted financial incentives reduced specialist attrition by 18% within 1 year

cards.big: Every specialist who leaves public service takes decades of taxpayer-funded training with them
Should government specialists receive competitive allowances to match private sector pay
